AreaLevelRanges.brief: A collection of constants defining different scope levels for area range configurations.
AreaLevelRanges.details: The AreaLevelRanges group encapsulates constant integer values that specify various scope levels used in area level and range configurations within the system. These constants include RANGE_OK for acceptable or default ranges, RANGE_ALL for encompassing all possible ranges, RANGE_CLAN for clan-specific areas, and RANGE_IMM for immediate or administrator-level ranges. Organizing these related constants together facilitates clear, consistent usage across the codebase and improves code readability and maintainability.
AreaLevelRanges.name: AreaLevelRanges

RANGE_OK.brief: Represents the value indicating a successful or acceptable range check.
RANGE_OK.details: The constant `RANGE_OK` is initialized to zero and is used as a sentinel value to signify that a range check has passed successfully. It is part of the `AreaLevelRanges` namespace or class, which defines various constants related to range validation. This constant is typically returned or compared against when verifying if a certain value falls within acceptable bounds, facilitating clear and consistent handling of valid states across the codebase.
RANGE_OK.name: RANGE_OK

RANGE_ALL.brief: Represents the constant value used to specify the 'all' range in area level configurations.
RANGE_ALL.details: The constant `RANGE_ALL` is initialized to 1 and is used to denote a range that encompasses all possible area levels within the application's context. It is typically employed in functions or data structures that require specifying a comprehensive or default range, ensuring that all areas are included. This constant is part of the `AreaLevelRanges` namespace or class, serving as a clear and maintainable way to refer to the 'all' range without using magic numbers. Its value is consistent across the codebase, facilitating readability and reducing errors when handling range specifications.
RANGE_ALL.name: RANGE_ALL

RANGE_CLAN.brief: The constant representing the specific range identifier for clan-related area levels.
RANGE_CLAN.details: RANGE_CLAN is a compile-time constant integer set to 2, used to denote the range category associated with clan areas within the application's level management system. It is part of the AreaLevelRanges namespace, which defines various range identifiers for different area types. This constant is utilized in functions and data structures that handle level ranges, ensuring consistent reference to clan-specific areas across the codebase.
RANGE_CLAN.name: RANGE_CLAN

RANGE_IMM.brief: The constant representing the range index for immediate (IMM) addressing mode.
RANGE_IMM.details: RANGE_IMM is a compile-time constant that specifies the index associated with the immediate addressing mode within the range classification system. It is initialized to 3, indicating its position or identifier in the set of predefined address range categories. This constant is used throughout the codebase to refer to the immediate addressing range consistently, facilitating clear and maintainable handling of different address modes.
RANGE_IMM.name: RANGE_IMM
